title: PC Control Tools
description: JSON Schema definitions for PC control tools available to the ideas-brillantes assistant. Enables direct interaction with the desktop environment: launching apps, capturing the screen, simulating mouse and keyboard input.

tools.name: open_app
tools.description: Launch a desktop application by name, optionally passing command-line arguments.

name.description: Name or executable path of the application to open (e.g. 'firefox', 'gedit', '/usr/bin/vlc').

args.description: Optional list of command-line arguments to pass to the application.

tools.name: close_app
tools.description: Gracefully close a running application by name. Sends a close signal; the application may prompt to save unsaved work.

name.description: Name of the running application to close (e.g. 'firefox', 'gedit').

tools.name: screenshot
tools.description: Capture a screenshot of the current desktop or active window.

path.description: Optional file path where the screenshot image will be saved (e.g. '/tmp/screenshot.png'). If omitted, a temporary path is used and returned.

tools.name: click
tools.description: Simulate a mouse click at the given screen coordinates.

x.description: Horizontal pixel coordinate to click (from the left edge of the screen).

y.description: Vertical pixel coordinate to click (from the top edge of the screen).

button.description: Mouse button to click. Defaults to 'left'.

double.description: If true, performs a double-click instead of a single click.

tools.name: type_text
tools.description: Type a string of text using simulated keyboard input at the current cursor position.

text.description: The text to type. Supports Unicode characters.

tools.name: hotkey
tools.description: Send a keyboard shortcut by pressing multiple keys simultaneously (e.g. Ctrl+C, Alt+F4).

keys.description: Ordered list of key names to press together (e.g. ['ctrl', 'c'] or ['alt', 'F4']). Key names follow the xdotool / pyautogui convention.
